page: Microsoft Azure E2d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-assisted virtualization, AVX-512 vector extensions, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 77 GB of local storage. Benchmark results reveal a significant performance divergence between single-core and multi-core operations. While single-core performance is average, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1889.4 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1462.0, multi-core performance is poor, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1723.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1933.38. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds registering at 10.7 GB/sec and 9.79 GB/sec respectively, both falling into the poor performance category.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and modest compute capacity, the E2d_v5 offers a specialized resource profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power, avoiding the need to provision larger, more expensive compute instances. However, its poor multi-core scaling, low memory bandwidth, and weak database operation benchmarks make it less suitable for heavy parallel processing, high-throughput databases, or intensive compilation tasks. Appropriate workloads include lightweight memory-bound applications, basic text processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and small-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E2d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical Intel Xeon 8370C core running at 2.8 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage. While single-core benchmarks like Geekbench and stress-ng show average performance, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are poor, with large-block read bandwidth at 10.7 GB/sec and a multi-core Geekbench score of 1723.0. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads by avoiding the expense of larger instances. However, its low memory bandwidth and poor multi-core scaling present clear tradeoffs. It is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, basic text processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and low-traffic web serving rather than intensive database operations or parallel computing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for lightweight, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM
77 GB of local storage bundled with the instance
Average single-core performance paired with poor multi-core scaling
High memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-bound applications
Best fit for lightweight text processing, single-threaded tasks, and basic web serving
